text: GParted shall allow users to create a partition table on a disk device.
GParted shall enable users to enable or disable partition flags such as boot, hidden, and lba.
GParted shall allow users to set or change a partition label.
GParted shall allow users to set or change a partition UUID.
GParted shall perform partition actions including creating, deleting, resizing, moving, checking, and labeling.
GParted shall support partition manipulation for file systems including btrfs, ext2, ext3, ext4, fat16, fat32, hfs, hfs+, jfs, nilfs2, ntfs, reiserfs, reiser4, ufs, and xfs.
GParted shall require a minimum of 256 MB of RAM to operate all features.
GParted shall function as a GNOME application.
GParted shall use libparted to detect and manipulate devices and partition tables.
GParted should provide an optional graphical user interface for ease of use.
The GParted application shall be licensed under the GNU General Public License.
GParted shall support the use of various storage devices including Hard Disk Drives, Flash Memory Devices, and RAID Devices.
